race.comment: He's taken a while to get to the races, but BAD BLOOD (2) has been a trial star for some time now and comes up with the inside alley for the debut. He's demonstrated an ability to take up a position when asked in his jumpouts, which has him poised to be prominent throughout. EIGHTEEN MONTHS (1) made a nice return at Warracknabeal on Apr 4, when he led but knocked up late and was still only beaten 1.5 lengths. He'll have come on from that and has drawn well. FINNISH GIRL (6) beat home EIGHTEEN MONTHS last start and while she doesn't have the same level of improvement to come as him and has drawn poorly, she only needs to hold that form to be a chance again. ELSA OF ARENDELLE (13) hasn't placed since the debut third but this is a step down on most of the races she's contested since, so expect her to be prominent. CALL ME CHARLIE (3) is the other debutant and has won his only two jumpouts racing on-pace, so he could give a sight.
